lenses.title: System map and hidden consumers
lenses.mission: Trace inputs, callers, state, outputs, consumers, deployment surfaces, and tests beyond the changed files.
lenses.question: Which dependency or consumer makes the local change behave differently at system scale?
lenses.disconfirm: Try to prove that every material upstream and downstream edge is already represented in the proposed impact surface.
lenses.evidence: Name inspected entry points, dependency edges, consumers, and tests with file or command references.

lenses.title: Contracts and invariants
lenses.mission: Extract explicit and implicit contracts, state invariants, error semantics, and compatibility promises.
lenses.question: What invariant could the change violate while all happy-path tests still pass?
lenses.disconfirm: Search for callers, schemas, fixtures, or documentation that contradict the inferred contract.
lenses.evidence: Reference the contract source and at least one boundary or failure path inspected.

lenses.title: Counterexample and abuse of assumptions
lenses.mission: Construct the smallest realistic counterexample to the plan, including malformed, stale, reordered, partial, or hostile inputs.
lenses.question: What is the cheapest input or event sequence that makes the intended result false?
lenses.disconfirm: Attempt the counterexample or show the exact guard that prevents it.
lenses.evidence: Provide a reproduction, test target, trace, or precise guard reference.

lenses.title: Root cause and reproduction
lenses.mission: Reproduce the failure, trace every relevant caller, and locate the earliest shared cause rather than patching one symptom.
lenses.question: Which causal mechanism explains the observed failure and its sibling paths?
lenses.disconfirm: Try to reproduce the symptom through another caller and falsify the proposed root cause.
lenses.evidence: Reference the reproduction command, failing observation, callers, and the shared correction point.

lenses.title: Independent verification oracle
lenses.mission: Define observations that distinguish success from plausible-looking failure without trusting the implementation's own claims.
lenses.question: Which independent test or measurement would fail if the implementation were subtly wrong?
lenses.disconfirm: Look for assertions that only restate implementation details or mock away the risky boundary.
lenses.evidence: Name exact commands, assertions, fixtures, measurements, and untested gaps.

lenses.title: Forced orthogonal frame
lenses.mission: Apply a deterministic frame that is deliberately different from the task's dominant framing.
lenses.question: What becomes visible after changing the representation or observer?
lenses.disconfirm: Try to show that the alternate frame adds no new causal surface.
lenses.evidence: Record the alternate frame, what it exposed, and the inspected evidence.

lenses.title: Security and privilege abuse
lenses.mission: Trace trust boundaries, identities, authorization, secrets, injection sinks, and attacker-controlled values.
lenses.question: How can an untrusted caller gain data, authority, execution, or persistence it should not have?
lenses.disconfirm: Search for bypass callers and validate guards at the final privileged sink, not only at the first entry point.
lenses.evidence: Reference sources, sinks, guards, identities, and a negative or abuse-path check.

lenses.title: Concurrency and ordering
lenses.mission: Inspect races, atomicity, locks, cancellation, duplicate work, ordering, and time-of-check/time-of-use gaps.
lenses.question: Which interleaving, retry, cancellation, or duplicate event breaks the invariant?
lenses.disconfirm: Construct a two-worker or reordered-event schedule and identify the synchronization that rules it out.
lenses.evidence: Reference shared state, synchronization points, queue semantics, and a concurrency-focused check.

lenses.title: Data lifecycle and recoverability
lenses.mission: Trace creation, validation, migration, mutation, retention, deletion, restore, and partial-failure behavior.
lenses.question: How can valid data become lost, duplicated, orphaned, stale, or irrecoverable?
lenses.disconfirm: Exercise partial writes, old records, retries, rollback, restore, and mixed-version data.
lenses.evidence: Reference schemas, transactions, migration order, retention behavior, and recovery checks.

lenses.title: API and consumer compatibility
lenses.mission: Check wire formats, public interfaces, version skew, caller assumptions, deprecation, and rollout order.
lenses.question: Which old or external consumer breaks even though the new producer works with itself?
lenses.disconfirm: Inspect real callers and test mixed old/new producer-consumer combinations.
lenses.evidence: Reference interface definitions, consumers, versions, fixtures, and compatibility checks.

lenses.title: Distributed failure and delivery semantics
lenses.mission: Inspect network partitions, retries, timeouts, duplicate delivery, eventual consistency, and remote partial success.
lenses.question: What happens when the remote side succeeds but the local side observes a timeout?
lenses.disconfirm: Model loss, duplication, delay, reordering, and split success across process boundaries.
lenses.evidence: Reference retry policies, timeout boundaries, delivery guarantees, reconciliation, and failure checks.

lenses.title: Performance and capacity
lenses.mission: Inspect algorithmic growth, fan-out, hot paths, allocation, caching, backpressure, and realistic load limits.
lenses.question: Which input size or traffic shape turns acceptable local behavior into a bottleneck?
lenses.disconfirm: Estimate or measure the worst plausible path instead of extrapolating from a tiny fixture.
lenses.evidence: Reference complexity, query counts, allocations, benchmarks, profiles, or capacity assumptions.

lenses.title: Operability, rollout, and rollback
lenses.mission: Check deployment order, configuration, observability, alerts, diagnosis, feature controls, rollback, and recovery time.
lenses.question: How will an operator detect, contain, and reverse a bad release under pressure?
lenses.disconfirm: Simulate partial rollout or rollback with mixed versions and stale configuration.
lenses.evidence: Reference deploy/config paths, logs, metrics, alerts, flags, runbooks, and rollback checks.

lenses.title: User journey and accessibility
lenses.mission: Trace real user goals, error recovery, accessibility, localization, devices, and slow or offline conditions.
lenses.question: Which realistic user can no longer complete the job despite the component looking correct?
lenses.disconfirm: Exercise keyboard, screen-reader, narrow-screen, localized, empty, slow, and error states as applicable.
lenses.evidence: Reference routes/components, interaction states, accessibility semantics, and end-to-end observations.

lenses.title: Dependency and supply-chain integrity
lenses.mission: Inspect dependency necessity, provenance, version constraints, lockfiles, transitive risk, and build reproducibility.
lenses.question: What new failure or trust boundary enters through this dependency or build change?
lenses.disconfirm: Check whether the platform or standard library already suffices and whether clean locked builds reproduce.
lenses.evidence: Reference manifests, lockfiles, advisories, licenses, build output, and the simpler alternative considered.

lenses.title: Maintainability and deletion pressure
lenses.mission: Challenge speculative abstraction, duplicated policy, hidden coupling, ownership, and unnecessary moving parts.
lenses.question: What can be removed or made explicit without weakening the contract?
lenses.disconfirm: Search for a second real use case before accepting a new abstraction or dependency.
lenses.evidence: Reference duplication, coupling, ownership boundaries, alternatives, and maintenance burden.

lenses.title: Cost and resource economics
lenses.mission: Estimate steady-state and failure-amplified compute, storage, network, model-token, and operator costs.
lenses.question: Which retry, fan-out, retention, or worst-case path multiplies cost unexpectedly?
lenses.disconfirm: Calculate an upper bound using realistic volume and failure assumptions.
lenses.evidence: Reference unit costs, volume assumptions, quotas, budgets, and a bounded estimate.

lenses.title: Privacy and compliance
lenses.mission: Trace personal or regulated data through collection, purpose, access, retention, export, deletion, and audit.
lenses.question: Where can sensitive data outlive its purpose or cross an unintended boundary?
lenses.disconfirm: Trace one record end-to-end, including logs, analytics, backups, exports, and deletion propagation.
lenses.evidence: Reference data fields, purpose, access controls, retention, deletion, audit, and applicable authoritative policy.
